rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving named things to valid expressions with types; delete invalid clauses if root references are unresolved.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes; move valid assigns to implementation scopes.
Ensure AGREE scalar types match AADL expectations: int, real, bool; avoid package-qualified identifiers not in the model.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are visible in the current scope and match the AADL model's structure.
Use only component type-level guarantees for visible ports; implementation-level bindings must be in implementation annexes.
Validate that AGREE expressions have correct types; align helper declarations with their referenced expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Guarantees and assumptions must reference valid expressions with known types.
Constants and equations in AGREE must use scalar types: int, real, bool.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the AADL model.
Do not place implementation-level subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about named things without types
Model Analyst Agent's role in extracting AADL structure
